Chinese clean energy expertise seen helping mitigate damage
Seraphine Mutindi, villager Makueni county, southern Kenya, used rely farming support her family six. However, things began change three ago.
"There little rain due changing weather patterns, land so dry I cannot even grow maize," Mutindi, 35, mother four, Kithina village, said.
Having abandon her farmland, less half hectare, she do manual jobs cleaning house, gathering firewood washing clothes earn $2 day buy food support her family.
make ends meet, they only meal, primarily ugali, locally popular staple made corn flour, noon every day.
Mutindi' situation worst village 35,000 people. 200 people village, mostly elderly who lost partial ability work, starved death past, she said.
Kenya just countries Horn Africa experiencing most severe drought past 40 induced climate change. Across Horn Africa, 36 million people affected prolonged drought end, including 4.5 million Kenya, result likely fifth consecutive failed rainy season, United Nations Office Coordination Humanitarian Affairs said month.
Worse, region may face sixth failed season between March May next, warned. Already 9.5 million livestock died across region, said.
visit Garissa county eastern Kenya month country' Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua appealed international aid help his country fight catastrophic drought.
" here today Garissa bring spotlight suffering being endured Kenyans result global climate crisis," said. " resources cannot sufficient address challenges climate change."
increasing impact climate change worldwide, 27th Conference Parties United Nations Framework Convention Climate Change, COP27, held Sharm El-Sheikh, Egypt, month, gained global attention.

Hong Kong striving develop renewable energy sources reduce carbon footprint, hydrogen poised play significant role combating climate change creating clean, green economy.
Electricity generation largest contributor carbon emissions Hong Kong, accounting 66 percent total carbon emissions 2019, according city' Climate Action Plan 2050.
special administrative region relying imported fuel generating power, electricity imported Chinese mainland, meet electricity demand. various initiatives drive development renewable energy, Hong Kong' fuel mix electricity generation changed.
2015, coal accounted 48 percent electricity generation, ratio dropped 24 percent 2020. same period, ratio natural gas fuel mix ballooned 27 percent 48 percent. Nuclear renewable energy' contribution had risen, although slightly - 25 percent 28 percent - between 2015 2020, according Climate Action Plan 2050.
HKSAR government expects renewable energy' share fuel mix generating power increase less 1 percent present between 7.5 percent 10 percent 2035, jump 15 percent subsequently.
Zero-carbon energy, renewable energy, refers energy sources generate little no carbon emissions production use. sources solar, wind hydrogen.
Hong Kong developing renewable energy sources fronts. government strive develop advanced waste--energy facilities generate electricity. reviewing proposals city' power companies develop offshore wind farms, encouraged community develop solar energy.
Waste accounted 7 percent city' total carbon emissions 2019, according Climate Action Plan 2050. Developing waste--energy facilities promoting waste reduction recycling mean shifting away reliance landfills municipal waste disposal leads high carbon footprint.
Hong Kong' drive develop large-scale renewable energy still faces constraints due geographical factors, scarce land resources, densely populated areas.
Among zero-carbon energy sources, hydrogen set play significant role efforts combat climate change.
Hydrogen versatile, nontoxic lightweight gas stored, transported converted clean power. importantly, potential decarbonize wide range sectors. clean-green economy developed when hydrogen feed range applications, chemicals, transport, buildings manufacturing, if produced large scale.
Grey hydrogen ( carbon-free), most common form, generated via carbon-intensive methods, using natural gas coal feedstock; green hydrogen (carbon-free) produced sustainable power sources via methods electrolysis - chemical reaction breaks water down constituent parts, hydrogen oxygen. Renewable power sources, solar wind, applied generate green hydrogen.
Hydrogen Council - international advisory body foster long-term clean energy transition - expects global hydrogen demand reach 546 million metric tons 2050, current 70 million metric tons, 6.4-percent annual growth rate.
Viable alternative
Among various business sectors, hydrogen offers viable alternative battery-powered electric vehicles. power sources being used Hong Kong transport vehicles still heavily reliant gasoline, diesel electricity.
Under Climate Action Plan 2050, SAR government pledged cut total carbon emissions 26 percent 36 percent 2030 when set against 2005 baseline. 2020, carbon emissions were 20 percent lower those 2005.
Lawrence Cheung Chi-chong, chief innovation officer Hong Kong Productivity Council, says Hong Kong should think how develop application hydrogen fuel cells public transport when hydrogen fuel cell technology cut greenhouse gas emissions technology emits only water. "There potential public transport vehicles, buses taxis, adopt hydrogen-enabled mode, private cars commercial vehicles like trucks vans likely adopt battery-powered model," says.
battery-powered electric commercial vehicles, like buses taxis, charging time usually around midnight when daily operations end, charging facilities needed provided there adequate land, Cheung explains. " Hong Kong' estimated 7,000 franchised public buses 18,000 taxis service, viable sustainable find adequate space facilities provide electric charging these vehicles overnight."
Unlike charging electric-vehicle batteries takes time, hydrogen refilling process completed few minutes. Most liquefied petroleum gas refilling stations upgraded hydrogen refilling stations easily.
time using private cars commercial vehicles standardized, easier owners these vehicles use electric charging, says Cheung, time refilling hydrogen buses taxis similar refilling gasoline-powered buses taxis.
Bravo Transport Holdings, owner Hong Kong' bus operators Citybus New World Bus, launched Hong Kong' hydrogen fuel cell double-decker bus July - months after introduced city' electric double-decker bus service. bus Hong Kong' -ever hydrogen fuel cell vehicle, world' tri-axle hydrogen fuel cell double-decker bus.

bus enter service after passed inspection testing processes relevant laws regulations updated. vehicle equipped fuel cell converts hydrogen electricity power bus entire system. able cope local operating environment, including high passenger loads, steep road gradients, long service time operating mileage, well constituent use air-conditioning systems.
" business value chain generated adoption hydrogen energy green transport involve research development, production, transportation, storage, refilling electricity generation. involve different expertise create types jobs," says Steven Chan Nai-kin, president Hong Kong Fuel Cell Hydrogen Energy Association.
Hydrogen-fueled cars require maintenance repairs differ those electric battery-run cars. They require expertise electrical, power chemical engineering hydrogen-filling stations need expertise protecting storage tank power transmission hydrogen prevent leakages.
SAR government rather passive encouraging hydrogen used another energy source, says Chan. " government dragging feet waiting breakthroughs hydrogen generation technology. does proactively deal issues associated transportation storage hydrogen energy."
Collaboration, supports needed

money used help supplier' poorest customers-those less 1,000 pounds savings who spend 10 percent disposable income fuel. Each qualifying home receive donations between 250 pounds 750 pounds.
company' move follows rival UK energy giant Scottish Power suggesting energy bills being effectively frozen customers, government picking tab cost expensive energy customers would otherwise pay. government, sofar, embraced idea.
Poverty activists criticized British Gas offering divert only 10 percent profits, some saying do little help average families struggling pay heating bills winter.

noted sum amounts just 1 percent parent company Centrica' total profits.
"They've got 7.5 million (residential) energy customers UK, so if you look money available, it' probably going benefit around 40,000 people max," said.

#280
Economists dusting off dreaded portmanteau word "stagflation" describe looming crisis threatens envelop eurozone.
Most well remember economic woes brought Great Recession 2007 2009, new challenge different ilk, said Iain Begg, professor London School Economics' European Institute.
" former financial crisis no surge inflation, different sort crisis largely emanating energy market. better parallel mid-1970s," Begg said.
conflict Ukraine increased gas costs, sending consumer prices soaring, efforts combat inflation slowed economic growth.
Economists define "stagflation " period slow economic growth, high unemployment inflation.
" scenario qualifies stagflation," investment firm UBS said recent client note, prompting flashbacks half century ago, when term popularized amid oil price crisis plunged United States much Europe recession.
UBS downgraded eurozone growth 2.7 percent 2.6 percent, European Commission recently said inflation eurozone hit 7.6 percent, major uptick May estimate 6.1 percent.
conflict Ukraine, coupled reliance Russian gas, key driver behind Europe' economic woes. main gas pipeline Russia reduced capacity low 20 percent various times summer, sparking fears total cutoff.
" (European Union) perma-crisis, implies very challenging period ahead," said Fabian Zuleeg, chief economist European Policy Center think tank. " how whether EU manages deal current challenges... depend difficult political choices coming months."
situation exacerbated turmoil region' largest economies, Germany Italy. month, relatively stable coalition government Italy upended infighting key relief bill combat inflation, culminating resignation Prime Minister Mario Draghi.
Italy since approved 17.4 billion euro ($17.85 billion) package shield households consumers inflation, recently decelerated historic high 8 percent, turmoil country remains headache European Central Bank, according Begg.
"There concern financial markets target Italy way,, obliges European Central Bank respond emphatically, second, leads renewed pressure relatively indebted eurozone members," Begg said.
Political upheaval Italy potential weaken European Union, should anti-EU parties manage succeed upcoming elections, early polling shows these factions gaining ground, said Angel Alonso Arroba, vice-dean school public affairs IE University Madrid.
week, Deutsche Bank said Germany staring down recession, Friedrich Merz, who leader German opposition party, Christian Democratic Union, recently said nation facing " worst economic crisis since founding Federal Republic Germany ( 1949)".
Arroba said, since Germany economic engine Europe, "recession there bad news rest (EU)".
"I am sure whether Germany heading worst crisis since founding,, certainly, difficult context world economy whole", marked inflation disruptive effects conflict Ukraine, added.
Germany trace current high reliance Russian gas back 2011 key decision phase down nuclear energy production following Fukushima disaster. Germany now scrambling diversify energy mix, new legislation promises expedite country' green energy transition doubling renewable power capacity next eight.
sense, Europe importing inflation experiencing moment. good news, according Begg, nations alleviate stress switching alternative sources energy. bad news take time, now, European community little choice "grin bear " when comes stagflation.
Arroba said: "Any response needs coordinated European level. Unity cohesion response crisis critical. If European countries engage competition among themselves, they make things worse."

addition, country consistently pushing forward research development large advanced pressurized water reactors high temperature gas-cooled reactors, numerous technological breakthroughs onshore commercial modular small reactor construction well numerous advanced nuclear energy systems, said.

China continues pushing forward goals peaking carbon emissions 2030 achieving carbon neutrality 2060, attention focused energy security, believed nuclear power sector see massive opportunities come.
China become largely self-sufficient numerous sectors, including reactor design construction, well aspects fuel cycle. working exporting nuclear technology rest world, said.

#283
potential global warming cause catastrophic, humanity-ending events "dangerously unexplored", according international team climate experts called governments institutions establish so-called climate endgame research agendas.
Public discourse around climate change often centered energy transition, keeping warming below 2 C. new paper journal Proceedings National Academy Sciences suggests neglect pessimistic view future peril.
"Climate change played role every mass extinction event," said lead author Luke Kemp University Cambridge' Center Study Existential Risk. " helped fell empires shaped history."
doomsday scenarios put forth researchers China, United Kingdom, Germany, United States involve climate targets being missed wide margins, well natural man-made disasters triggering series cascading events.
Kemp said paths disaster limited direct impacts high temperatures, extreme weather events.
"Knock- effects, financial crises, conflict, new disease outbreaks could trigger calamities, impede recovery potential disasters nuclear war," said. " catastrophic risk there, need detailed picture."
team urged Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change dedicate future report catastrophic climate change, hopes galvanize research inform public. report should consider outcomes ranging 10 percent loss global population eventual human extinction.
Kristie Ebi, epidemiologist University Washington who contributed research, said greater appreciation catastrophic climate scenarios help compel public action.
"Understanding nuclear winter performed similar function debates nuclear disarmament," Ebi said.
Authors paper proposed new research agenda includes what they call "four horsemen" climate endgame: famine malnutrition, extreme weather, conflict, vector-borne diseases.
Thirty million people already live under average annual temperatures 29 degrees Sahara Gulf coast, meanwhile 20 million people forced leave homes move areas countries each due resource scarcity extreme weather events, according United Nations.
" 2070, these temperatures social political consequences directly affect nuclear powers, seven maximum containment laboratories housing most dangerous pathogens," said Xu Chi, researcher Nanjing University China who co-authored paper. "There serious potential disastrous knock- effects."
Modeling done team shows areas extreme heat - annual average temperature 29 C - could cover 2 billion people 2070, many them living densely populated politically fragile areas.

Central banks' efforts contain high rising inflation fueling growth headwinds threatening tip global economy recession. proximate cause today' inflationary pressures large, broad-based persistent imbalance between supply demand.
Higher interest rates dampen demand, supply-side measures must play large role strategies tame inflation.
past so, rollback containment policies amid COVID-19 pandemic spurred simultaneous surge demand contraction supply. expected, supply proved surprisingly inelastic. labor markets, example, shortages become norm, leading canceled flights, disrupted supply chains, restaurant closures challenges healthcare delivery.

transition generate some inflationary pressure. Yes, nominal prices wages limited downward flexibility. time excess demand, companies generally try pass higher costs via price increases- they often get away, least.

today' inflation deeper roots. past several decades, activation massive amounts underutilized labor productive capacity emerging economies generated deflationary pressures. those resources having now significantly depleted, relative prices many goods set rise.

case fossil fuels, previous pattern underinvestment capacity multiple points along supply chain compounded problem.
there even story. 75 percent world' GDP produced countries aging populations. Old-age dependency ratios rising, some countries, workforce shrinking. Productivity gains could counter contraction labor supply relative demand, after nearly decades falling productivity growth, gains forthcoming.
So inflation rising fast, central banks under pressure take drastic action. only real option reduce demand, raising interest rates withdrawing liquidity. These measures already spurred massive repricing assets, including currencies, they threaten push global growth below potential- lower-income economies suffering disproportionately- reduce investment energy transition.
However, there another way: supply-side measures. Trade investment long enabled supply expand rapidly response growing global demand. nearly decades- especially few -proliferating trade barriers adding friction process. European Parliament voted Wednesday back proposal label natural gas nuclear energy "green" despite strong opposition many lawmakers environmentalists decrying plan "greenwashing".
proposal label gas nuclear power plants climate-friendly investments circulated European Commission December part so-called EU Taxonomy.
motion block proposal Wednesday received only 278 votes favor among MEPs, 328 against 33 abstentions. total 353 votes were needed order reject proposal 705-seat parliament.
majority MEPs center-right European People' Party, largest group parliament, majority centrist Renew Europe Group supported new labeling, most MEPs Greens Social Democrats opposed commission proposal.
Now member states decide fate. Unless 20 27 EU member states against proposal, become law bloc next.
European Commission welcomed results vote. statement, said gas nuclear now included EU Taxonomy transitional activities limited number circumstances under strict conditions.
Mairead McGuinness, European commissioner charge financial services, financial stability capital markets union, said proposal pragmatic ensure private investments gas nuclear, needed energy transition, meet strict criteria.
"Investment renewables already prioritized taxonomy," she said.
Thomas Waitz, MEP Austria co-chair European Green Party, mocked proposal.
"If gas nuclear sustainable, fries salad," wrote tweet right before Wednesday' vote.
Austria Luxembourg already threatened sue European Commission green labeling European Union court.

Key EU member states were split initially proposal, support voiced France rejection Germany.
France 56 nuclear reactors generates 70 percent electricity nuclear energy, highest world. Germany still moving forward promise phase out nuclear power plants end, despite renewed debate issue amid worsening energy crisis. German Green Party, key player coalition government, long argued environmental hazards caused disposal nuclear waste far exceed benefits.

cost nuclear power coastal China competitive coal half cost building new nuclear units United States, United Kingdom France, according Alex Whitworth, research director energy resources consultancy group Wood Mackenzie.

plan calls wider use nuclear energy heat residential industrial areas desalinate seawater. places, Haiyang, Shandong province, Haiyan, Zhejiang province, were successful achieving commercial nuclear heating 2021-22 heating season.

British Prime Minister Boris Johnson told nuclear industry officials March government wanted country obtain 25 percent electricity nuclear power, would signal significant shift country' energy mix. past few, nuclear produced between 15 20 percent nation' energy.
French government announced plan build 14 additional nuclear reactors fleet smaller nuclear plants seeks slash greenhouse gas emissions cut reliance foreign energy. France derives 70 percent electricity nuclear energy, highest percentage world.

nation' federal government submitted draft so-called Easter Package, bundle legislative measures aimed developing renewables, including reforms country' Renewable Energy Act Off shore Wind Law.
crisis Ukraine added extra impetus renewable energy transition, since Germany burns natural gas around 15 percent electricity, Russia main supplier.
Finance Minister Christian Lindner recently referred renewable power "freedom energy".
Highlighting need reform, Germany' economy minister, Robert Habeck, said news conference "climate crisis coming head" conflict Ukraine "shows how important phase out fossil fuels promote expansion renewables".
new legislation aims increase share renewables Germany' energy mix, around 42 percent 2021 80 percent 2030. package commits Germany sourcing electricity renewables 2035. Previously, German government had said would achieved "before 2040".
Wind energy play large part transition, aim reaching 30 gigawatts installed off shore wind capacity 2030, least 70 gigawatts 2045.
Onshore wind capacity set reach 115 gigawatts 2030, according new legislation, compared current 56 gigawatts.
Germany currently around 7.7 gigawatts installed off shore wind capacity, third place behind United Kingdom China, though development slowed recent.
nation added 219 megawatts offshore wind power 2020, no off shore wind turbines. Germany issued fewer onshore wind permits quarter same period.
Habeck, economy minister, told reporters Tuesday low growth wind power sector odds proposed reforms Easter Package.
" want change," said. " unsatisfactory."
package largely welcomed green-energy industry players environmentalists.

" only emphasizes risks fossil dependence shared across economies reeling increasingly severe impacts carbon pollution," Constantino said.
latest report, International Energy Agency said global power generation coal expected jumped 9 percent, following economic rebound boosted electricity demand. Asia dominates global coal market.

price cap, however, falls way below current market prices hit much $250 per ton, pushing miners flout rules. Local supply aggravated weather related disruptions reduced coal production.

Warming target 1.5 C looms challenge century
stakes stratospherically high heading 26th United Nations Climate Change Conference Parties, COP 26, opens Glasgow Sunday.
World leaders, industry heads, academics, celebrities climate campaigners gather Scottish city weeks intense debate global warming.
event diplomatic meet, trade expo focal point demonstrations rolled.
President Xi Jinping said climate change "nature' alarm bell humanity", there broad consensus among attending delegations alarm now deafening.
United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson, who hosting COP 26, described conference turning point mankind, United States climate envoy John Kerry said meeting " best chance" avoid environmental catastrophe global scale.
Glasgow, leaders negotiate how best achieve accelerate goals laid out Paris 2015, when agreement keep global warming below 2 C, preferably 1.5 C, struck.
success COP 26 largely hinge what countries agree terms domestic targets, well cross-border mechanisms financing curb emissions.
Ahead COP 26, most nations submitted domestic goals emissions reductions, known Nationally Determined Contributions, NDCs.
However, negotiations Glasgow, there huge "elephant room", sum these NDCs sufficient close so-called emissions gap.
Climate Action Tracker estimates stay course 1.5 C warming mid-century, world must collectively reduce emissions 20 billion 23 billion metric tons those made annually 2030.
NDCs submitted serve narrow gap just 4 billion tons, according Tracker, independent scientific analysis produced research organizations tracking climate action since 2009.
Separate estimates recently released UN found total emissions reduction 55 percent 2030 needed keep 1.5 C target sight, current NDCs world course mere 7.5 percent reduction.
Adair Turner, member House Lords UK who chairs Energy Transitions Commission, ETC, said, ", most major emitters committed achieve net-zero emissions mid-century.
" getting closer objective. if you look NDCs, submitted ahead COP 26, they far adequate give even 50/50 chance limiting global warming 1.5 degrees, 90 percent chance limiting 2 degrees."
UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres called " ambition every country" NDCs, current targets set world "catastrophic pathway" 2.7 C global warming.
" must stop. must reversed," said recent Pacific Islands Forum.
Ambitious pledges
Some world' major economies made ambitious emissions reductions pledges, including net-zero targets, months leading conference.
2030, UK aims reduce emissions least 68 percent compared 1990 levels. committed halving emissions 2030, China pledged peak carbon dioxide emissions before 2030 achieve carbon neutrality before 2060.
China' special climate envoy Xie Zhenhua said virtual news conference Oct 19 nation would "fight hardest" achieve emissions neutrality targets coming decades. said COP 26 provides opportunity construct "road maps actionable policies measures" ensure countries "honor words real deeds".
Tuesday, State Council, China' Cabinet, released action plan carbon dioxide peaking before 2030., share non-fossil energy consumption around 25 percent, carbon dioxide emissions per unit GDP drop 65 percent compared level registered 2005, according plan.
Asher Minns, executive director Tyndall Centre Climate Change Research University East Anglia UK, said net-zero pledges impact despite being voluntary non-binding.
"Anecdotally, I would say net-zero ambitions focus minds, some backed policy action economic incentives- UK example," Minns said.
Net-zero pledges major economies appear placed pressure industry well some slow movers emissions reductions.
Australia, example, criticized submitting NDC unchanged previous commitments. nation now committed net-zero target 2050, Prime Minister Scott Morrison saying modeling outlining emissions reductions released "eventually".
Turner, ETC, said net-zero pledges very important, "once government committed net-zero target, company commitments follow".
cited example China Baowu Steel Group Corp, committed achieving emissions neutrality 2050, immediately following China' 2060 net-zero pledge.
" company now developing clear plans get there, progress already 2020s," Turner said.
Ajay Gambhir, climate economist Imperial College London, said absence pledges India, world' third-largest emitter, would surely talking point COP 26.
"India yet submit new NDC, most glaring thing right now, current NDC easy meet," Gambhir said. "India made no net-zero target announcement yet either, so many eyes."
Delegates conference tasked negotiating cross-border action climate change.
Financing crucial
Patricia Espinosa, executive secretary UN Framework Convention Climate Change, said COP 26 only claim success if global climate financing targets met.
2015, developed nations agreed raise $100 billion annual financing support developing countries mitigate effects climate change phase out fossil fuels. target yet met, delegates expected negotiate new 2025 target floor $100 billion.
Gambhir said: " money critical, even though there' clear rationale tackling climate change, there still costs borne. If there aren't clear credible plans finally provide money, then increase later decade, won't track steer emerging economies off fossil fuels, makes 1.5 C much harder."

point international cooperation remains unresolved Article 6 Paris climate agreement, covers carbon markets mechanisms incentivize emissions reductions. Failure reach agreement Article 6 cast shadow negotiations COP25 Madrid.

"Developed countries should take lead reducing emissions. They should pin responsibilities onto China developing nations," Zheng said.
